brief: Draft a mailing-list signup page headline and two supporting sentences for the pre-launch site. Voice must match founder profile; no luxury framing.
outcome_summary: Delivered three variants with different cadences. Founder picked variant 2; variants 1 and 3 read too polished.
rating_notes: Good register. Variant 2 hit; 1 and 3 were over-smoothed.

brief: Draft a pitch email to VinePair's Pennsylvania wine coverage editor announcing the first vintage is in barrel.
outcome_summary: Drafted a pitch. Founder rejected; said pre-release press is not the plan and the manager should have confirmed before drafting.
action_items: Confirm press strategy (pre- vs post-release) before drafting outreach
rating_notes: Wrong assumption. Founder has flagged press strategy as an open question. Manager should have asked, not drafted.

brief: Write a 80-word shelf-talker for the Nine Shades Pét-Nat that substantiates every claim against the technical sheet. Flag anything that would need verification.
outcome_summary: Delivered 78 words with three flagged claims: 'picked at 18.5 Brix' needs harvest-log verification, 'spontaneous ferment' is substantiated by cellar notes, 'no sulfur at primary' is substantiated but the final bottling SO2 needs to be added before print.
action_items: Confirm final SO2 ppm at bottling with operations
rating_notes: Exactly the right behavior: flagged its own unverified claims.

brief: Build a first-vintage COGS model for the two 2026 SKUs. Include fruit cost, crush fee, dry goods, and direct-labor time. Flag assumptions.
outcome_summary: Model delivered with 11 named assumptions and three calibration points against comparable producer data. Nine Shades Pét-Nat lands at $14.80/bottle COGS; Caldwell Hollow Cab Franc at $17.40/bottle. Both have 8% sensitivity bands.
rating_notes: Clear, substantiated, caveated correctly. This is the standard.

brief: Draft the narrative portion of a PA Agriculture Industry grant application for a $28,000 facility-adjacency award. Focus on the rural-producer qualification criteria.
outcome_summary: Narrative drafted against all four qualification criteria. Clean cross-reference to the funding-request section. One inference flagged: manager assumed the facility-adjacency criterion covers custom-crush arrangements, which should be confirmed with PLCB before submission.
action_items: Confirm with PLCB that custom-crush counts as facility-adjacency per the grant program criteria
rating_notes: Strong substantiation, appropriate caveat. Manager clearly read the program criteria.

brief: Draft a provisional pick schedule for the three contracted vineyards assuming average 2026 ripening data. Build in two-week rolling updates from Penn State extension data.
outcome_summary: Schedule delivered covering a 42-day window for all three vineyards. The Caldwell Hollow and Slate Run windows overlap on 3 days, which is manageable. The Nine Shades window starts 12 days before either other vineyard, so no conflict there.
action_items: Confirm with vineyard owners that the provisional windows work for their own harvest commitments
rating_notes: Good. The overlap-flagging is the right instinct.

brief: Outline the exact sequence of TTB COLA submissions for the two 2026 SKUs. Include lead times and dependencies on label finalization.
outcome_summary: Sequence outlined but with a compressed timeline that assumed TTB would process in 21 days. Actual processing times have been running 35-42 days for wine COLAs in 2026; the submitted plan doesn't account for this.
action_items: Rework sequence with 42-day TTB processing assumption
Add 2-week buffer for potential re-submission after corrections
rating_notes: Wrong on the lead time. Used outdated TTB processing-time data. Critical-path implication is significant; could delay release by a month. Manager knows.
